notes.note: "Veteran Antistius" renders "veteris Antisti" — likely Publius Antistius Vetus, whose cognomen Vetus ("old"/"veteran") Brutus plays on; he had held an independent command (hence imperator) and was standing for the praetorship of 42 BC.

notes.anchor: falling in with Dolabella in Achaia
notes.note: P. Cornelius Dolabella, declared a public enemy by the Senate in early 43 BC, was passing through Greece with troops on his way to Syria; he later besieged and captured Smyrna, where he killed C. Trebonius.

notes.anchor: twenty thousand sesterces out of his own purse
notes.note: Twenty thousand sesterces (HS XX) was a significant but not enormous private donation; by comparison, a legionary soldier's annual pay was about 900 sesterces, so this represented roughly 22 years of an ordinary soldier's wages.

The praetorian comitia were the electoral assemblies (comitia centuriata) that voted for praetors, the second-highest magistracy in the cursus honorum; holding the praetorship was a prerequisite for the consulship and for commanding provincial armies.

notes.anchor: the time of his standing for office
notes.note: Brutus urged Antistius not to postpone his candidacy for the praetorship; the elections for 42 BC would be held later in 43 BC, and a delay in announcing one's candidacy could cost a man votes and political momentum.
